title: calandrinia pilosiuscula var. tenella
intro: Calandrinia pilosiuscula var. tenella, also known as Tenella Poppy or Rock Purslane, is a low-growing, succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Calandrinia pilosiuscula var. tenella include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in topical applications to promote wound healing and skin health.
